ai_chat.query: With the map of byron bower how much percent is contaminated

response.content: The contamination issue in Bryn Bower, primarily caused by Exafluoran leakage from Hallman Inc., has a significant impact on geographical areas within the city.
The prohibited zone for water extraction, which has Exafluoran concentrations exceeding 20 ppb, encompasses a large part of the city area.

response.title: Map of Bryn Bower indicating the contaminated areas
response.description: A detailed overview of the different pollution zones in Bryn Bower, showcasing the spread and impact of Exafluoran contamination.

content: According to the 2020 status data, approximately 30% of the city, including key areas like downtown and Bryn East, falls under the water extraction prohibition zone that exceeds 20 ppb.

response.content: This widespread contamination affects residential areas and possibly the commercial zones, posing health risks and impacting property values.

response.title: (Leaked Confidential Document) Hallman Cleanup Cost Estimates
response.description: Insights into the financial and procedural challenges faced by Hallman Inc. in addressing the Exafluoran contamination.

content: The ongoing clean-up efforts and strategic plans involved substantial financial outlay, yet they aim to manage only critical areas rather than achieving comprehensive remediation across all affected zones.

response.content: It's evident from the map and cleanup strategies that the contamination issue remains a pressing concern, despite extensive remediation efforts.

response.title: Hallman Plume Scandal; Tumultuous BB Townhall Meeting
response.description: A record of the public sentiments and the heated debates surrounding the contamination issue and Hallman’s proposal at a townhall meeting.

content: The polarization within the community is significant, with some residents vehemently opposing the Hallman settlement, reflecting deep mistrust and worries about long-term environmental and health implications.
